name: Dong Zhuo (Zhongying)
page_title: Dong Zhuo (Zhongying) 董卓 (仲穎) [Han, Dong Zhuo] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: General of the Gentlemen of the Household of the East
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Dong Zhuo was not historically saved by Liu Bei, Zhang Fei and Guan Yu while fighting the Yellow Turbans.
	Dong Zhuo was never presented with the “Seven-Star Sword” by Cao Cao after a failed assassination attempt.

sections.premium-external-linkage: Dong Zhuo’s Sanguozhi Biography A rough-draft work-in-progress by Lu Kang.
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Dong Zhuo, styled Zhongying, was from Lintao county, Longxi commandery. He was the second son of Dong Junya.

In 189, Dong Zhuo seized control of the imperial court using his army. He deposed Liu Hong, who was Excellency of Works, to take his title for himself. He became discontented with Emperor Shao and soon deposed him in favor of Emperor Xian, who became his puppet. He later became Grand Commandant after moving Liu Yu, who held that title, to Grand Major.

In the first lunar month of 190, several men in administrative posts across the realm formed a coalition under Yuan Shao and waged war on Dong Zhuo. Dong Zhuo soon moved the capital from Luoyang to Chang’an, sacking and burning Luoyang. He tried to send a peace delegation to the coalition, but all but one were killed.

full_text:
Dong Zhuo (Zhongying) 董卓 (仲穎)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Dong Zhuo (董卓), styledZhongying (仲穎), was born in Lintao, Longxi. Seized control of Luoyang and the Han government, and governed ruthlessly. Dong Zhuo was affiliated with the Han Dynasty. Dong Zhuo lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Dong Zhuo, styled Zhongying, was from Lintao county, Longxi commandery. He was the second son of Dong Junya.
In 189, Dong Zhuo seized control of the imperial court using his army. He deposed Liu Hong, who was Excellency of Works, to take his title for himself. He became discontented with Emperor Shao and soon deposed him in favor of Emperor Xian, who became his puppet. He later became Grand Commandant after moving Liu Yu, who held that title, to Grand Major.
In the first lunar month of 190, several men in administrative posts across the realm formed a coalition under Yuan Shao and waged war on Dong Zhuo. Dong Zhuo soon moved the capital from Luoyang to Chang’an, sacking and burning Luoyang. He tried to send a peace delegation to the coalition, but all but one were killed.
